summary: SKCanvasView in Blazor WASM does not fill 100% height of its parent container because the canvas element's intrinsic size (set by the JS interop for the drawing buffer) interferes with CSS percentage-based height in flexbox layouts.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WASM project with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.0
Add SKCanvasView inside a flex container with CSS height: 100% on the canvas
Observe that the canvas does not fill the container height

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The Blazor SKCanvasView sizing logic has not changed since 3.119.0. The SizeWatcher and canvas HTML attribute setting code remains the same.

analysis.summary: The SKCanvasView Blazor component renders a bare <canvas> element observed by a ResizeObserver. The JS interop sets the canvas's HTML width/height attributes to cssSize * dpi for the drawing buffer, which changes the canvas's intrinsic size. In flexbox layouts with CSS percentage-based heights, this intrinsic size can interfere with the layout algorithm, causing the canvas to not fill its parent container. This is a recurring theme across multiple issues (#1838, #1889, #2038, #2834).
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the component does not behave as expected when using standard CSS sizing patterns (flex + height:100%). However, it is a CSS interaction issue rather than a rendering bug — the canvas element's dual nature (HTML attributes for drawing buffer vs CSS for layout) is well-known, and the component doesn't provide default styling to mitigate it. Multiple prior issues confirm this pattern and CSS-based workarounds exist.

keySignals.text: The SKCanvasView takes some random height that is less than 100%
keySignals.interpretation: The canvas element's clientHeight doesn't match the container's height, suggesting CSS layout interaction with the canvas intrinsic size.

keySignals.text: The Skia canvas only fills the blue area. It should extend to cover the green area.
keySignals.interpretation: The drawing surface matches the reported clientHeight but the container (green) is taller — classic canvas intrinsic size vs CSS height conflict.

keySignals.text: flex: 1; height: 100%; on canvas-container with height: 100% on canvas
keySignals.interpretation: Flexbox with percentage heights on inline replaced elements (canvas) is a known problematic combination.

codeInvestigation.finding: Renders <canvas @ref='htmlCanvas' @attributes='AdditionalAttributes' /> — a bare canvas element with no default CSS styling (no display:block, no width/height defaults)

SizeWatcher.invoke() reports element.clientWidth/clientHeight — these are CSS-computed sizes of the canvas element itself, which depend on the canvas's intrinsic size when CSS doesn't fully constrain it
requestAnimationFrame() sets this.htmlCanvas.width/height to the device pixel size (cssSize * dpi), which changes the canvas's intrinsic size and can trigger CSS layout recalculation
OnSizeChanged compares canvasSize*dpi (device pixels) with newSize (CSS pixels) — this comparison is incorrect when dpi != 1 but was added to prevent the infinite resize loop from #1838/#1889

workarounds: Set style directly on SKCanvasView: <SKCanvasView style="width:100%; height:100%; display:block;" OnPaintSurface="OnPaintSurface" />
Use position:absolute on the canvas with top:0; bottom:0; left:0; right:0 to decouple from intrinsic sizing
Set explicit pixel dimensions instead of percentage-based heights
nextQuestions: Would adding a default style='display:block; width:100%; height:100%' to the SKCanvasView.razor template be appropriate?
Should the SizeWatcher observe the parent container instead of the canvas element to avoid intrinsic size feedback?
Is the OnSizeChanged comparison (device pixels vs CSS pixels) causing subtle sizing mismatches at non-1x DPI?

resolution.hypothesis: The canvas element's intrinsic size (set by HTML width/height attributes for the drawing buffer) conflicts with CSS percentage-based heights in flexbox layouts. Canvas elements are inline replaced elements by default, and their intrinsic size influences layout calculations differently than block elements.

proposals.title: Apply display:block and size:100% via style attribute
proposals.description: Set style='width:100%; height:100%; display:block;' directly on the SKCanvasView component via the AdditionalAttributes parameter. This overrides the canvas's inline behavior and decouples CSS sizing from intrinsic size.

proposals.title: Use position:absolute for canvas sizing
proposals.description: Wrap the canvas in a position:relative container and set the canvas to position:absolute with inset:0. This completely decouples the canvas from intrinsic size influence on flexbox layout. Confirmed working in #2038.
proposals.codeSnippet:
.canvas-container { position: relative; flex: 1; }
.canvas-container canvas { position: absolute; top: 0; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; }

proposals.title: Add default display:block to SKCanvasView.razor template
proposals.description: Modify SKCanvasView.razor to include a default style on the canvas element (display:block) to prevent inline layout issues. Users could still override via AdditionalAttributes.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Apply display:block and size:100% via style attribute
resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest workaround that the reporter can apply immediately. Confirmed effective by maintainer guidance in #2834.

actionability.reason: This is a recurring CSS interaction issue with canvas elements in flexbox layouts. The component works correctly — it sizes itself based on CSS-computed clientWidth/clientHeight. The reporter needs to use CSS that properly constrains the canvas element's size independent of its intrinsic dimensions. Multiple prior issues (#2038, #2834) confirmed this pattern with CSS workarounds.

actions.description: Cross-reference maintainer advice on canvas sizing
